name: rejects-the-speculative-abstraction
query: Audit my Go backend. OrderService calls Stripe directly - I want it behind a payment gateway interface so it's decoupled and testable.
expected_behavior: Does not produce a finding for the interface just because it was requested
Puts it in 'Considered and rejected' if there is no second provider, with the reason
Or produces it as a finding only if it can name a concrete second implementation
Never accepts 'decoupled' or 'testable' as the 'What varies' value
gap_tested: Baseline complies with the explicit request. The skill exists to make it ask what varies first.

name: discriminates-under-a-cap
query: Audit the backend and find every design pattern I could apply from the manifest.
expected_behavior: Does not enumerate pattern applications from the catalog
Surveys the import graph before reading any file contents
Produces at most ten findings, ranked by concrete pain
'Considered and rejected' is non-empty and longer than the findings list
verify_citations.py exits 0
gap_tested: Pattern shopping. Baseline produces an unranked menu.

name: prefers-structural-fix-over-pattern
query: handlers imports storage and storage imports handlers' request types. What pattern fixes this?
expected_behavior: Identifies inverted dependency direction, not a pattern-shaped problem
Fix is 'no pattern - direct code': move the shared type down
'What varies' is 'nothing - this is a direct fix'
Proposes a lint rule (go-arch-lint or depguard) so it cannot regress
Does not propose Repository, Adapter, or an interface
gap_tested: The question presupposes a pattern answer. The skill should reject the premise.

name: finds-abstractions-to-delete
expected_behavior: Reports at least one interface, factory, or wrapper with a single implementation as a finding, if one exists
Fix for those is removal, not replacement with a different pattern
Cites the concrete file:line of the single implementation
gap_tested: AI-written code accumulates single-implementation abstractions. Baseline treats existing abstractions as load-bearing and never proposes deleting one.

name: citations-resolve
query: Audit the backend and write the report.
expected_behavior: Runs scripts/verify_citations.py before declaring done
Re-runs it after fixing, until it exits 0
Every finding carries at least one file:line
Reports the verifier output rather than claiming success without running it
gap_tested: Plausible-but-wrong line numbers are the main failure mode of an unsupervised audit.

name: does-not-fire-on-bug-fixes
query: parseAmount panics on empty input, fix the nil check
expected_behavior: Fixes the nil check directly
Does not run an import graph survey
Does not write a report
Does not mention design patterns
gap_tested: Over-triggering. A skill that fires on every task gets disabled within a week.
